mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing present values of future cash flows and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.05) across 100+ rate cut observations, or Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.4 percentage points within a single month signals abrupt labor market deterioration, triggering recession fears that force immediate equity deleveraging and negative forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or the signal fails to predict direction during historical unemployment spikes.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and business confidence, driving durable demand growth that lifts corporate earnings expectations and yields positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to produce positive 60-day returns in > 60% of cases, or interaction term loses significance in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp contractions in industrial production during periods of high inflation indicate supply chain bottlenecks and capacity constraints, forcing firms to cut margins and triggering immediate short-term equity sell-offs.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during high CPI periods do not predict negative 5-day returns, or the mechanism shows positive returns instead.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate level above 4.5% signals aggressive monetary tightening that increases discount rates and compresses valuation multiples, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain positive in > 50% of observations when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5%, or Rank IC is negative over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and rising household income, which supports corporate revenue growth and generates positive equity returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth fails to correlate with 60-day forward returns (Rank IC < 0.01) across multiple business cycles, or positive payroll surprises yield negative returns.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between slowing GDP growth and declining retail sales signals weakening aggregate demand that forces immediate earnings downgrades, triggering rapid short-term equity liquidation and negative weekly returns.
failure_condition: The negative interaction between GDPC1 and RSAFS deviations does not predict negative 5-day returns, or the signal shows positive predictive power instead.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained increases in industrial production during low-inflation regimes reflect healthy manufacturing demand and operational efficiency, lifting corporate earnings forecasts and generating positive monthly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth during low CPI periods fails to predict positive 20-day returns, or the signal reverses direction during historical disinflation episodes.
